chapter: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: Solve the following system of linear equations graphically: $x - y = 1$ and $2x + y = 8$. Determine the coordinates of the vertices of the triangle formed by these two lines and the $x$-axis.

steps: Find at least two solutions for each equation to plot the lines.
Plot the lines on a graph. The intersection point of the two lines is $(2, 1)$.
The line $x - y = 1$ meets the $x$-axis at $(1, 0)$ and the line $2x + y = 8$ meets the $x$-axis at $(4, 0)$.
The vertices of the triangle are $(2, 1)$, $(1, 0)$, and $(4, 0)$.
solution.derivation: $x - y = 1 \Rightarrow y = x - 1 \text{ (Points: } (1, 0), (2, 1)) \\ 2x + y = 8 \Rightarrow y = 8 - 2x \text{ (Points: } (4, 0), (3, 2)) \\ \text{Intersection: } x - 1 = 8 - 2x \Rightarrow 3x = 9 \Rightarrow x = 3 \text{ (Wait, recalculating intersection: } x-1 = 8-2x \Rightarrow 3x = 9 \Rightarrow x=3, y=2) \\ \text{Correction: } x - 1 = 8 - 2x \Rightarrow 3x = 9 \Rightarrow x = 3, y = 2. \text{ Intersection is } (3, 2). \\ \text{The lines meet the x-axis at } y=0: x-0=1 \Rightarrow x=1 \Rightarrow (1, 0) \text{ and } 2x+0=8 \Rightarrow x=4 \Rightarrow (4, 0). \\ \text{Vertices are } (3, 2), (1, 0), (4, 0).$
